name: Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime
name_original: Ruta de los entornos de Grandas — circular desde Grandas de Salime
region_intro: Hiking around Grandas De Salime offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by mountains, deep valleys, and rich forestland. The region features the prominent Grandas de Salime Reservoir, fed by the Navia River, which also forms meanders with its tributary, the Agüeira River. Altitudes typically range between 500 and 650 meters, providing varied terrain for exploration. The area is also a key point on the Primitive Way of St. James, offering significant elevation changes and scenic views. Best hiking trails around Grandas De Salime The most popular hiking route is Agüeira River Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This path follows the Agüeira River, offering views of the local waterways. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This circular route explores the surroundings of Grandas de Salime, showcasing the varied local environment. Local hikers also love the Berducedo to Buspol, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. This route is part of the Camino Primitivo, offering significant elevation changes. Hiking around Grandas De Salime is defined by the Grandas de Salime Reservoir, the Navia and Agüeira rivers, and the surrounding mountains and forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Grandas De Salime are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Grandas De Salime's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Grandas de Salime?
A: Grandas de Salime offers a diverse network of over 25 hiking routes. These trails traverse varied terrain, from river valleys to mountain paths, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Grandas de Salime features 5 easy hiking routes. These trails are generally less strenuous, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. The region's varied terrain, with altitudes typically between 500 and 650 meters, ensures scenic views without overly challenging climbs on these easier paths.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Grandas de Salime?
A: Hikers in Grandas de Salime will encounter diverse natural landscapes, including mountains, deep valleys, and rich forestland. Key features include the expansive Grandas de Salime Reservoir, the meandering Navia and Agüeira rivers, and the gentle slopes of the Protected Landscape of the Sierras de Carondio and Valledor, known for their lush forests.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Grandas de Salime?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047545115/route-of-the-environments-of-grandas-circular-from-grandas-de-salime" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.5 km path that explores the varied local environment around the town. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047547680/route-of-mon-and-green-path-of-san-martin-de-oscos-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of Mon and Green Path of San Martín de Oscos — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.5 km long.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I explore near the hiking trails?
A: Grandas de Salime is rich in history and culture. You can visit the ancient Chao Samartín Hillfort and Museum, which offers insights into Roman gold mining. The Ethnographic Museum of Grandas de Salime ('Pepe el Ferreiro') provides a deep dive into traditional Asturian rural life. Additionally, the Collegiate Church of San Salvador, with its Romanesque elements, is an important stop on the Primitive Way of St. James.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see while hiking in the area?
A: Yes, the region boasts several beautiful waterfalls. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Murias Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5341997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queixoiro Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, all offering picturesque natural sights along various routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Grandas de Salime?
A: The climate in Grandas de Salime is generally pleasant, with moderate rainfall and dry, warm summers. This makes the summer months ideal for hiking. However, the varied forestland and diverse flora also offer beautiful scenery during spring and autumn, with vibrant colors and cooler temperatures.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The mountains and forests of Grandas de Salime are home to various wildlife. Hikers might spot wild boar, roe deer, and foxes. Hares and partridges are also present in the area, particularly in the more secluded natural environments.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Grandas de Salime?
A: The hiking experience in Grandas de Salime is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the scenic views of the reservoir and rivers, and the opportunity to experience sections of the historic Primitive Way of St. James.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Agüeira River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047548912/agueeira-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agüeira River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 7.6 km route that specifically follows the Agüeira River. This trail offers pleasant views of the local waterways and the surrounding natural environment.

Q: Can I hike a section of the Camino Primitivo (Primitive Way of St. James) in Grandas de Salime?
A: Yes, Grandas de Salime is a key point on the Primitive Way of St. James, serving as the last Asturian stage before pilgrims enter Galicia. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048357206/berducedo-to-buspol" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berducedo to Buspol&lt;/a&gt; route, an 8.5-mile (13.7 km) trail, is part of this historic pilgrimage, offering significant elevation changes and scenic views through mountainous terrain.
tips: A church well worth seeing, which should definitely be visited in the afternoon when it is open. There is also a pilgrim stamp here.

The ethnographic museum of Grandas de Salime has been in charge, since 1984, of conserving and disseminating all the historical and cultural heritage of the western communities of Asturias. Open every day except Monday, and general admission costs 1.50 euros.

Grandas de Salime is a council in the western region of the Principality of Asturias. It has less than 900 inhabitants and is famous, above all, for the Salime reservoir. On the Primitive Way, it is the last stage before entering Galicia.

waypoints.name: Collegiate Church of San Salvador in Grandas de Salime
